name: v1.7.1
body: Added branded README with logo, usage examples, and exports table to all 10 npm packages
Added LICENSE to all sub-packages for npm compliance
Complete translations: Japanese, Chinese, Spanish, French, Hindi, Italian, Portuguese
All 10 packages now live on npm at v1.7.1:
| Package | npm |

name: v1.7.0 ã Supply Chain Receipts
body: Three new receipt pipelines for security teams and executives:
registry-sync** ã Prove what's published vs what's in the repo
Drift classification: in sync, ahead, behind, unpublished
Each package becomes a receipt output with version comparison
security-audit** ã Prove what you scanned, with what tool, and what was found
Severity breakdown (critical/high/moderate/low/info) in intent and metadata

name: v1.6.0 ã Bundle Authority
body: Bundles can now be signed and their signatures verified, establishing **who created the parcel**. The zip contents are never mutated ã signatures live as detached sidecars (`.sig`/`.cert`) next to the bundle.
`rf bundle sign <file>`** ã Sign a bundle with cosign (detached sidecar)
`--keyless` for OIDC-based signing in CI
`--key <path>` for local key-based signing
`rf bundle verify --require-bundle-signature`** ã Require a valid bundle-level signature
`rf verify <file.zip> --require-bundle-signature`** ã Auto-detect passthrough

name: v1.5.0 ã Receipt Bundles
body: v1.5.0 introduces **receipt bundles** ã self-verifying zip archives that package a receipt with its entire trust chain into a single portable file.
bundle.zip/
ããã manifest.json     # Bundle metadata
ããã hashes.json       # SHA-256 of every file
ããã VERIFY.md         # Human-readable verification instructions

name: v1.4.0 ã Strict Refs, Policy Identity, Policy Signing
body: Default stays best-effort ã missing references are reported but don't fail verification. With `--refs-strict`, missing or unreadable references fail. Hash mismatches always fail regardless of mode.
```bash
rf verify receipt.json --follow --refs-strict --receipts-dir receipts/

Receipts now embed `policy_identity.hash` ã the SHA-256 of the lint policy rules applied at creation time. During verification, if a policy is supplied, the hash is compared. Mismatch = fail. This prevents policy swapping.
`PolicyIdentity` type: `hash`, optional `version`, `path`, `signed`, `signatureRef`

name: v1.3.0 ã Multi-Tenant SaaS Architecture
body: App template upgraded** to `/app/[workspace]/ãÎ` multi-tenant routing (3 seeded workspaces)
RBAC policy layer** ã `canViewBilling`, `canManageTeam`, `canManageWorkspace`
Feature flags** ã `billing`, `teams`, `auditLog`, `apiKeys` with `isEnabled()` gating
Centralized data layer** ã workspace-keyed projects, metrics, and activity
Path-preserving workspace switcher** with plan badges (starter / pro / business)
AccessDenied component** + billing page RBAC gate

name: v1.0.1 ã Handbook + Publish Workflow
body: HANDBOOK.md** ã complete operational reference (13 chapters + 3 appendices) covering architecture, commands, performance model, Docker execution, resource governance, security, tuning, troubleshooting, and design decisions
Publish workflow** ã 3-channel automated release (PyPI via OIDC, GHCR gateway image, GitHub Release artifacts)
```bash
pip install --upgrade claude-toolstack-cli

name: v1.0.0 ã Bounded Code Intelligence
body: A ready-to-deploy stack that keeps Claude Code productive on large, multi-language repositories without thrashing a 64-GB Linux workstation.
FastAPI gateway** with 6 bounded endpoints (search, slice, ctags, symbol, job runner, metrics)
systemd cgroup v2 slices** ã 5 slices enforce MemoryHigh/Max per service group
Exec-only Docker model** ã socket proxy blocks 14 higher-risk Docker endpoints
`cts` CLI** ã zero-dependency Python client with text/JSON/Claude output modes
Evidence bundles v2** ã 4 bundle modes (default, error, symbol, change) with path ranking + git recency

name: v1.0.4
body: First release with full RepoMesh evidence pipeline:
CycloneDX SBOM generated and attached
SLSA-style provenance statement attached
Signed ReleasePublished event broadcast to ledger
This release should converge to trust score 100/100 in the network.

name: v0.4.5
body: Added 82 new tests covering previously untested modules:
`formatters.test.ts` ã all observability format* functions (44 tests)
`run-manager-pure.test.ts` ã OOM detection patterns, duration formatting, run state (27 tests)
`metadata.test.ts` ã path helpers, provenance index, run metadata loading (11 tests)
Exported `isOomError` and `formatDuration` from run-manager for testability
Full suite: **187 tests**, all passing (was 105)

name: v0.5.5
body: Added 80 new unit tests covering previously untested modules:
`comfy-server-engine.test.ts` ã constructor, buildWorkflow logic (21 tests)
`job-router.test.ts` ã run ID generation, workspace keys, index creation (17 tests)
`ffmpeg-utils.test.ts` ã cleanupPartialVideo, interface shapes (10 tests)
`types.test.ts` ã constants, type contracts, schema guards (17 tests)
Full suite: **235 tests**, all passing (was 155)

name: v0.2.1
body: Added 7 new test files covering previously untested modules:
Logger** (9 tests): message formatting, log levels, error detail handling
UI Primitives Extended** (27 tests): list, input, select, checkbox, tabs, accordion, dialog, image, custom
UI Builders** (31 tests): searchResults, dashboard, wizard, confirmation, statusDisplay, emptyState, errorDisplay
Config Provider** (9 tests): workspace detection, config loading, event emission
Extension Lifecycle** (9 tests): activation, command registration, disposables, deactivation

name: v0.3.4
body: Added 8 new test files covering schema validation, config loader, nvidia/rocm probes, GPU probe dispatch, governor edge cases, experiment store, token bucket, and resource monitor
Fixed pre-existing bug in `init.test.ts` where `minFreeRamGB` was referenced under `gpu` sub-object instead of at root schema level
Added `rocm` to GPU probe enum in config schema
Wired rocm probe into dispatcher with auto-fallback chain: nvidia-smi ã rocm-smi ã null
Committed previously untracked source files: `rocmProbe.ts`, `format.ts`

name: v0.3.1
body: 102 unit tests** across 7 test files covering all extension modules
Vitest configuration with comprehensive vscode module mock
Exported `parseToolResult` and `formatVttTime` for testability

name: v0.2.0
body: 62 Python unit tests** for CLI core ã coverage report parsing, diagnostics, staleness checks, file discovery
37 TypeScript unit tests** for VS Code extension ã CLI output parsing, JSON validation, diagnostic publishing, CLI discovery
CI integration** ã unit test steps (pytest + vitest) added to CI workflow
CLI discovery converted from blocking `execSync` to async `execFile`
Typo `bySevertity` ã `bySeverity` in results tree provider
Extension version aligned with CLI engine (both now 0.2.0)

name: v0.1.2
body: readmeTranslator** (37 tests): segmentReadme parser, cleanTranslation quirk removal for 10+ languages, translateReadme integration
util** (15 tests): getConfig, pickLanguage/pickLanguages, log, friendlyError with contextual error detection
commands** (17 tests): translateSelection, translateFile, checkStatus ã edge cases, cancellation, error paths
statusBar** (9 tests): all 4 polling states (ready, Ollama down, model missing, connection error)
statusTree** (10 tests): tree provider states, dispose cleanup, model filtering
Exported `segmentReadme` and `cleanTranslation` for testability

name: v0.1.1
body: First published release of Polyglot for VS Code ã local GPU translation powered by TranslateGemma 12B via Ollama.
Translate Selection** ã Select text, press `Ctrl+Alt+T`, pick a language
Translate File** ã Translate an entire file into a new `file.ja.ext` alongside the original
Translate README** ã Batch-translate README.md into 7 languages, preserving code blocks, tables, and badges
Sidebar Panel** ã Globe icon in the activity bar with action buttons and live Ollama status
Status Bar** ã Real-time Ollama connection status with auto-polling
